input.command:
find /workspace -type f -printf '%s %p
' | sort -rn | head -10 | awk '{printf "%-10s %s
", $1, $2}' | while read size path; do du -h "$path" | awk '{print $1}' | tr '
' ' ' && echo "$path"; done
find /workspace -type f -exec ls -lh {} \; | awk '{print $5, $NF}' | sort -hr | head -10

final_response:
Here are the 10 largest files in /workspace/ with their sizes in human-readable format:

| Size | File Path |
|------|-----------|
| 340 bytes | /workspace/data/large-export.csv |
| 213 bytes | /workspace/data/backup.sql |
| 146 bytes | /workspace/logs/app.log |

It appears there are only 3 files in the /workspace directory. The workspace contains:
- A CSV export file (340 bytes)
- A SQL backup file (213 bytes)  
- An application log file (146 bytes)
